commands.review_summary:
The PR enables Bedrock Nova prompt-cache eligibility, preserves direct Nova cacheRetention through the agent path, suppresses Claude-only one-hour TTL for Nova cache points, and updates docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +65, Tests +220, Docs +1. Total +286 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source behavior: current main forces direct non-Anthropic Bedrock through no-cache, while PR head routes Nova through cache-point eligible paths and preserves explicit retention. No live AWS Nova request was reproduced in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer live AWS Nova proof plus maintainer acceptance of the upgrade behavior, not a safe automated repair task.

target.title: feat(bedrock): enable prompt caching for Nova models
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #29707. Amazon Nova generation models on Bedrock were still treated as unsupported non-Anthropic models, so OpenClaw forced `cacheRetention: "none"` and never emitted Bedrock `cachePoint` blocks for Nova requests.

## Why This Change Was Made

AWS documents explicit prompt caching support for Amazon Bedrock models, including Nova generation models: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-caching.html

This change teaches the shared Bedrock cache policy to recognize Nova Micro, Lite, Pro, Premier, and Nova 2 Lite refs, lets direct Nova requests keep their cache settings, and keeps the existing no-cache fallback for unsupported non-Anthropic Bedrock models. For Nova, `cacheRetention: "long"` emits the default Bedrock cache point without reusing Claude's `ttl: "1h"` field.

The follow-up commit also preserves configured direct Nova `cacheRetention` values through the normal agent extra-params path, including the explicit `cacheRetention: "none"` opt-out.

## User Impact

Users can enable prompt caching for Bedrock Nova text models without hitting the previous runtime no-cache override. Unsupported non-Anthropic Bedrock models, such as Mistral, continue to be protected from unsupported cache metadata.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.cache-retention-default.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.cache-retention-default.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/amazon-bedrock/index.test.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/stream.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`

Live Bedrock Nova request proof was not run because this Codex environment does not have AWS Bedrock credentials. Redacted live `Converse` or `ConverseStream` proof is still needed before merge.

AI-assisted contribution.
